usage: Code Health rule overrides, picked up by CodeScene from the repo root. The **/ent/** rule set silences ent-generated code (client.go, mutation.go, *_create/update/query/delete.go, predicates, hooks, migrations): it is regenerated by 'go generate' and never edited by hand, so its complexity is not actionable tech debt. Caveat: the glob also covers the hand-written ent/schema/** files; schema files are small declarative structs, so losing analysis there is an accepted trade-off (rule sets support only one path each and globs have no negation). The sesame builder.go rule set silences 'String Heavy Function Arguments' only: it is a fluent module/command builder DSL whose string arguments (module and command names, event types, aliases) ARE the API surface, not primitive obsession, so wrapping them in value objects would hurt readability rather than help.

rules.name: Lines of Code in a Single File
Number of Functions in a Single Module
